MARCOS

# MARCOS

### Dadiwala Fauj

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

May 29, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Tthe**“Dadiwala Fauj”**or the Bearded Army by terrorists, India’s own marine unit, the [MARCOS](https://www.quora.com/What-was-the-coolest-military-unit-of-all-time/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2?__filter__=5&__nsrc__=notif_page&__sncid__=15388184525&__snid3__=21830356439), one of the deadliest military units in the world, famed for it’s fighting capabilities.

MARCOS, which was previously called as the Marine Commando Force, gets it’s current moniker from the short form for Marine Corps. Capable of undertaking amphibious military operations in any terrain, they have proven themselves even in Jammu and Kashmir with their operations in the Jhelum River and Wular lakes. They also undertake counter insurgency operations, often mingling with the local populace, and this is the reason why they are so much feared by the terrorists.

MARCOS was established in 1987, by the Indian Navy, which felt the need for a separate amphibious force. These men undergo some of the most rigorous training in the world ever, working on the latest technologies, weaponry and a gruelling training schedule, that can leave you totally exhausted. Initially they were trained by the US Navy Seals, and later the British SAS.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xiH0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F647183fc-27d3-4ec0-b293-14b46e85aac2_602x403.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xiH0!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F647183fc-27d3-4ec0-b293-14b46e85aac2_602x403.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

With their Russian counterparts.

**Selection and Training**

Primarily males from Indian Navy in their early 20s, they are first picked via an extremely tough selection process. First there is a 3 day long physical fitness and aptitude test, where 80% of the candidates are filtered out. And then you have something called the “Hell’s Week” where for the next 5 weeks, the candidates are put through some of the most extreme endurance test. This includes sleep derivation, extreme physical exercises, and only if you are actually able to make it during this time, you get selected for the training. It is literally a trail by fire, and only the fittest make it, no wonder they end up as one of the deadliest force.

If you believe you have made it now to the hallowed circle, just wait till the training starts. It covers a period of six months, and around 50% get filtered out in the first month itself, where the selected candidates undergo grueling physical tests. The next 9 months is when they learn how to use different weapons, intelligence gathering and also special warfare techniques. They are also trained in close combat techniques like Krav Maga, as well as counter insurgency warfare in any environment. One of the most grueling programs is something called the “Death Crawl”, where the trainees have to wade through 800 m of thigh high mud, loaded with 25 kg of gear, and a 2.5 km obstacle course. And if that is not enough, after this grueling exercise when the trainee is totally exhausted, he has to do the final test, shooting a target 25 m away, take that.

These trainees are taught to operate with all kind of weapons ranging from knives, crossbows to sniper rifles, handguns to bare hands. They are taught skydiving, operation of submersible craft, operations from submarines, operating submersible crafts. Forget about James Bond, Jason Bourne and Jack Ryan, these guys are the real deal. And they do all this without the luxury of having retakes.

Here are some of the feats these guys do

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4gVK!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F663338bc-c9d9-4b8a-a25b-afbd5b40a789_602x378.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4gVK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F663338bc-c9d9-4b8a-a25b-afbd5b40a789_602x378.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

And if you look at their track record, here are some major operations they have taken part in.

Operation Pawan(1987)- Helped in securing the harbors of Jaffna and Trincomalee during the 1987 IPKF operations.

Operation Cactus(1988)- Successfully foiled a coup attempt in Maldives.

Operation Rakshak- Counter insurgency operations in Jammu and Kashmir on Wular Lake and Jhelum River.

Operation Tornado(2008)- Attack on the terrorists during 26/11
